questions.text: How does the Surah begin regarding praise?
questions.answer: All praise is for Allah, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and to Him belongs praise in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What was the unique miracle given to Prophet Dawud (David) regarding the mountains and birds?
questions.answer: They were commanded to repeat Allah's praises along with him.

questions.text: What material was made 'pliable' (soft) for Dawud (AS)?
questions.answer: Iron, so he could make coats of armor.

questions.text: What was the speed of the wind subjected to Sulayman (AS)?
questions.answer: Its morning journey was a month and its afternoon journey was a month.

questions.text: What was the 'fountain' given to Sulayman?
questions.answer: A fountain of molten copper.

questions.text: How did the Jinn find out that Sulayman (AS) had died?
questions.answer: They only realized he was dead when a creature of the earth (termite) ate his staff, and he fell down.

questions.text: What lesson did the Jinn learn from the death of Sulayman?
questions.answer: That if they had known the Unseen, they would not have continued in the humiliating punishment (their labor).

questions.text: What was the 'Sign' for the people of Saba (Sheba) in their homeland?
questions.answer: Two gardens, one on the right and one on the left, in a land that was good and fertile.

questions.text: How were the people of Saba punished for their ungratefulness?
questions.answer: Allah sent upon them the flood of the dam (Arim) and replaced their lush gardens with gardens of bitter fruit, tamarisks, and some lote trees.

questions.text: What was the specific 'wish' of the people of Saba that led to their downfall?
questions.answer: They asked Allah to make the distance between their journeys longer, thus acting unjustly to themselves.

questions.text: Does Iblis (Satan) have any authority over mankind?
questions.answer: No, he has no authority; his influence is only to distinguish those who believe in the Hereafter from those who are in doubt.

questions.text: What happens to the 'argument' between the weak and the arrogant on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The weak will blame the arrogant for misleading them, and the arrogant will say they only followed what was presented to them; both will be in punishment.

questions.text: Can wealth and children bring one closer to Allah?
questions.answer: No, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds; for them is a manifold reward.

questions.text: What is the 'Truth' described as in Verse 49?
questions.answer: 'Say, The Truth has come, and falsehood can neither begin [anything] nor repeat [it]'.

questions.text: What is the 'single advice' Allah gives to the disbelievers in Verse 46?
questions.answer: To stand up for Allah, in pairs and individually, and then reflect that the Prophet is not mad.

questions.text: What is the 'great distance' mentioned in the final verse?
questions.answer: The distance between the disbelievers and the faith they wish to have once the punishment arrives; it will be too late.

questions.text: Who are the 'few' among Allah's servants mentioned in Verse 13?
questions.answer: The grateful ones (Ash-Shakur).

questions.text: Does Allah know the weight of an atom in the heavens or earth?
questions.answer: Yes, nothing escapes Him, not even something smaller or larger than that.

questions.text: What will the disbelievers say when they are presented with the punishment?
questions.answer: They will try to believe, but a barrier will be placed between them and what they desire.

questions.text: What is the 'Saba' mentioned in the title?
questions.answer: A kingdom in ancient Yemen known for its prosperity and eventual destruction due to disbelief.
